items.title: 'The party had to end': Young investors torn on budget tax reform

children.content: Some young Australians locked out of the property market due to unaffordable housing have pursued ETFs and "rentvesting" as a way to build financial security. Here's what they make of the budget's CGT reforms.

items.title: Should a nurse pay the same tax rate as their employer? The budget says no

children.content: As the federal budget prompts howls of protest from those who earn the bulk of their income from owning assets rather than working, we shouldn't forget the relative privilege that has allowed many of those people to take those risks.

items.title: Man charged with murder after woman and two children found dead in Sydney home

children.content: A man, 47, has been charged after three people were found dead in a home in Sydney's south-west in what police allege is a domestic violence-related incident.

items.title: Trump says he has postponed a planned new strike on Iran

children.content: US President Donald Trump says an attack on Iran that was "scheduled for tomorrow" has been postponed at the request of gulf state leaders.

children.content: A shooter has opened fire at a San Diego mosque and police believe people have been shot, authorities say.

items.title: 'I wish I were dead': The heartbreaking toll of Australia's favourite pastime

children.content: Australians are the world's biggest gamblers, losing billions every year. But the true cost goes far beyond the financial.

items.title: Stranded school students rescued from flooding Gold Coast national park

children.content: Almost 50 high school students and teachers have been rescued after becoming stranded by floodwater at a national park in the Gold Coast hinterland.

items.title: The parts of Victoria adding the biggest share to disaster damage bills

children.content: At least $57 billion worth of infrastructure in Victoria is vulnerable to more frequent and severe bushfires, floods and extreme heat. This figure is expected to grow.

children.content: The Tesla boss accused OpenAI of wrongfully trying to enrich investors and insiders at the nonprofit's expense, and failing to prioritise AI's safety.

items.title: 'Two years to live': Why we used our super for weight loss surgery

children.content: Weight loss surgery has become an increasingly common option for people grappling with obesity — and many are funding it using retirement savings.

items.title: A secret mouse experiment in the 1990s threatened Brisbane's water supply

children.content: Brisbane was days away from running out of drinkable water in the 1990s due to a secret mouse experiment that was kept hidden from the public … until now.

items.title: Overseas company makes billions in profit but is receiving millions in government bailouts

items.title: PM knocks back $94 million proposal to help flooded farmers restock

children.content: Widespread flooding in north-west Queensland killed more than 90,000 head of livestock this year, but farmers will have to go it alone if they want to rebuild.

items.title: There's no cure and no treatment for MND, but finally there is hope

children.content: Natalie has seen her body and speech deteriorate; she has lost her independence and dignity — but she says new research into motor neurone disease has given her hope for those diagnosed in the future.

items.title: Spanish PM's Socialists party on notice after historic defeat in stronghold

children.content: Voters in the region of Andalusia have sent the ruling Spain's Socialists yet another warning, adding to concerns Pedro Sánchez's government could suffer an electoral wipe-out in a nationwide vote.

image.altText: Pedro Sanchez on a campaign stage clapping and smiling.

items.title: Journalist in London stabbed at behest of Iran, court told

children.content: Pouria Zaratifoukolaei, who works for a Persian-language outlet critical of the Iranian government, was attacked near his home in London in 2024.

items.title: The dead fish waste disaster that Tasmania failed to plan for

children.content: High-volume salmon mortalities last year swamped Tasmania's waste streams as companies scrambled to find somewhere to accept them, documents show. There are now concerns the state remains unprepared for future mass deaths.

items.title: It's time for Eric Abetz to tackle Tasmania's debt. How far will he go?

children.content: The lead-up to state budget day is normally a time for flashy funding announcements. But as Tasmania stares down the barrel of $7 billion of debt, things are looking a bit bleaker.

items.title: Behind the front lines of the war against illegal waste

children.content: While Queensland councils cleaned up enough illegally dumped waste to fill more than 1,200 garbage trucks, investigators say they are getting better at catching those doing the wrong thing.

items.title: 'Thriller' history book about the bark petitions wins $50k literary prize

children.content: The 1963 Näku Dhäruk or Yirrkala bark petitions led to Australia's first land rights law. A new book about them just won two prizes at the NSW Literary Awards.
